**Diagrams**
![Civil 3D - Overview/Ke](https://sattarfd.github.io/image-host/sof_046_civil-3d-overview-key-_1.png)

- Overview
- Civil3D is a civil design CAD package used for roading, earthworks, pipes and survey.
- Survey
- Civil3D is the primary CAD package used for
- delivery
- of topographical surveys.
- Data adjustment is done prior to import into Civil3D, with Civil3D used for creation of points, strings and DTMs, and presentation of data.
- Key contacts
- Role
- Name
- Specialisation
- Software manager
- Expert
- Caleb Baildon
- Survey
- Expert
- Innes Hutchison
- Survey template
- Inquiry Tool
- Analysis> Inquiry
- Point inverse> gives bearing and distance between two points(Hide/Unhide)
- Panorama
- Can initiate by event view in Home> Palettes

**Transparent commend**
- Allow you to lunch this commend while performing other commend

**Project explorer**
- Home> Palettes> Project explorer
- Purple colures are editable

- Installation

**Configuring for survey**
- On the Menu bar go to Survey>Edit User Settings
- Click import user settings
- Navigate to
- `Water Team\02_Civil3D\99_Survey`
- To set Civil3D to start with the survey drawing template, enter the command 'OP' (for options).
- In Files>Template Settings>Default Template File Name for QNEW. Go to
- `C:\Users\ausfd0\OneDrive - WSP O365\5- DesignAid\Civil 3D\WSP - Q Drive\civil3d\templates`
- and chose the survey template for your Civil3D version.

- To create a New Zealand Transverse Mercator (NZTM) projection in Autodesk Civil 3D, you can use the following steps:
- In the command line, enter "MAPCSCREATE"
- Select Coordinatesystem
- Choose "Start with a coordinate system" and then Next
- Select "Projected" as the coordinate system type and then Next
- On the "Create a new Coordinate System from existing one"

- Figure Prefix database (Civil 3D uses the prefixes (like "CURB" for curb line) to automatically connect points )
- Toolspace> Survey> Figure Prefix Databases
- Linework code set
- Toolspace> Survey> Linework code sets
- Make sure "Automatic begin on figure prefix match" is ticked yes
- Toolspace> Survey> survey databases> New local> Right click on import event>
- Nothing for Network
- Import option
- Location for the Figure and linework

_Captured Tuesday, 31 December 2024 9:50 pm · Computational › Civil 3D_

- Modify the Display Settings
- Prospector
- Right-click the surface name → Select Edit Surface Style.

**Go to the Display tab**

**Turn OFF the layers for**
- Triangles
- Grid
- Points
- TIN (Triangular Irregular Network) edges
- Ensure that Contours (Major) and Contours (Minor) are toggled ON.
- You can adjust colors and linetypes for better visibility.
- Click OK.
- Edit the Surface Style
- select Surface Properties

**In the Surface Properties dialog box**
- Under the Information tab, find the Surface Style dropdown menu.
- Change the style to one with "Contours" in its name (e.g., "Contours 1 and 5 (Background)").
- Click OK.
- Use the Add Labels Tool

**Go to the Annotate Tab**
- Select Add Labels from the Labels & Tables panel.

**Choose Surface Labels**
- Select an appropriate Label Style (e.g., "Contour Label Line").
- Click Add.
- Label the Contours

**For Multiple Contours**
- After selecting Contour - Multiple, click in the drawing area and drag a line across the contours you want to label.
- Civil 3D will place labels along all the intersected contours.

**For Single Contour**
- After selecting Contour - Single, click directly on the contour you want to label.
- Adjust Label Appearance
- Toolspace/ Setting/ Surface/ Label style/ Edit
- Turn the background mask off if the text is not clear
- T

_Captured Thursday, 18 January 2024 11:56 PM · Computational › Civil 3D_

**Diagrams**
![4b- Creating Surface -](https://sattarfd.github.io/image-host/sof_063_4b-creating-surface-fr_1.png)

- Open your Civil 3D Project: Start Civil 3D and open the DWG file containing the contour lines.
- Open the ToolSpace: On the left side, you should see the ToolSpace. If it's not there, you can open it by clicking on the "Home" tab > "Palettes" panel > "ToolSpace".

**Create a New Surface**
- In the ToolSpace, go to the "Prospector" tab.
- Right-click on "Surfaces" > "Create Surface".
- Name the Surface: In the "Create Surface" dialog box, give your surface a name and description. Choose the appropriate style (e.g., "Contours 1' and 5'") and render material if needed. Click "OK".

**Add Contour Data to the Surface**
- In the ToolSpace, under "Prospector" > "Surfaces", you should now see your new surface listed. Expand it.
- Right-click on "Contours" > "Add". This will allow you to add the contour data from your DWG to the surface.
- Select the contour lines from your DWG. Once selected, press "Enter".
- Rebuild the Surface: After adding the contour data, the surface will automatically rebuild. You can see the surface in the Object Viewer or by using various surface styles.
- Review and Edit if Necessary: Once the surface is generated, you might need to make edits or adjustments. You can do t
